latin: Frodo et Sam soli ad terram Mordoris iter faciebant. terra ante eos vasta et desolata erat, plena cinerum et saxorum acutorum. caelum semper nubibus obscuris tectum erat, et aer foetidus spirabat. Sam, quamquam valde timebat, dominum suum numquam reliquit. cibum et aquam inter se dividebant, et lente per terram hostilem progrediebantur. Frodo pondus anuli in collo magis magisque sentiebat, sed finem itineris, Montem Fati, in animo semper tenebat.

questions.prompt: What was the land of Mordor like?
questions.target: Vast and desolate, full of ashes and sharp rocks
choices: Vast and desolate, full of ashes and sharp rocks

questions.prompt: How did Sam behave during the journey?
questions.target: Though afraid, he never left his master
choices: Though afraid, he never left his master
He took the Ring from Frodo

questions.prompt: What did Frodo feel more and more?
questions.target: The weight of the Ring on his neck
choices: The weight of the Ring on his neck

questions.target: He kept the end of the journey, Mount Doom, always in mind
choices: He kept the end of the journey, Mount Doom, always in mind

translation: Frodo and Sam were making their way alone to the land of Mordor. The land before them was vast and desolate, full of ashes and sharp rocks. The sky was always covered with dark clouds, and the air blew foul. Sam, although he was very afraid, never left his master. They divided food and water between themselves and slowly advanced through the hostile land. Frodo felt the weight of the ring on his neck more and more, but he always kept the end of the journey, Mount Doom, in mind.
